steps.title: Fastest check: compare a table or graph
steps.content: For an equivalent-expression question, enter the original expression and the answer choices in a calculator table or graph. The correct choice must match for several inputs, not just one. The matching choice is D, $15{x}^{3}+6{x}^{2}+4x+6$.

steps.title: Algebra check
steps.content: Use the work below to confirm the identity for every allowed input. This matters because one random matching input is only a quick screen, not a proof that two expressions are always equivalent.

steps.title: Line up like terms
steps.content: Adding polynomials keeps the signs shown in each group, then matching powers of $x$ can be combined.

steps.title: Combine each power of x
steps.content: The cubic terms give $$8x^3+7x^3=15x^3.$$ The quadratic term is $6x^2$, the linear term is $4x$, and the constants give $$9-3=6.$$ So the expression is $$15x^3+6x^2+4x+6.$$

steps.title: Confirm the choice
steps.content: That expression matches Choice D — $15{x}^{3}+6{x}^{2}+4x+6$, so D is correct.
